bias_direction: Session bias toward declaring B.0 closed under a broader cohort interpretation than the handoff stated. The Cohort-vs-Handoff section is honest about the 2.3× factor and the alternative narrower-interpretation availability, but the headline framing (`B.0 closed`) presupposes the broader interpretation is correct. Owner directive 2026-05-23 confirmed the operational application stands without PI amendment (the DR is the standing reference); the narrower-cohort exit ramp remains via per-slug counter-migration. A future session that narrows the cohort would need to reopen B.0 and selectively unbanner. Additional B.11 bias: the 'log mining without auto-INSERTing 173 discoveries' decision is self-protective — it limits this session's blast radius but defers work that the skill's §4 strict reading calls for. The discovery-surface artifact preserves replayability so the deferral is auditable; an outside reviewer would object that the skill calls for INSERT, and counter that PI rule #10 demands per-row verification before INSERT is safe.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An outside reviewer would object that the session record claims closure on a workplan item the owner sized at ~30 BPCs while actually touching ~70. The closure claim is contingent on the DR's cohort definition, which is itself authored by the same session. The same session also added then withdrew a PI queue entry within ~30 minutes — symptomatic of insufficient up-front deliberation. On B.11 the reviewer would push back on the choice to log backward-mined sources without inserting discoveries to evidence_sources — that is the letter of the skill (§4 'If new: INSERT into evidence_sources + source_slug_links'). Counter: PI rule #10 demands metadata_quality + verification_status for any row that will support synthesis; 173 unverified INSERTs would reintroduce exactly the AUTHOR-TITLE-ONLY pile the 2026-05-23 rehab session just closed. The discovery-surface artifact preserves every reference for replayable triage. The conservative read matches the 2026-05-11g prior session pattern.
